name: Italy
b2g_notes: All invoices to public administrations must transit through SDI. Codice Univoco d'Ufficio required as recipient identifier.
b2b_notes: All domestic B2B invoices must transit through SDI. Codice Destinatario or PEC address used as recipient identifier.
b2c_notes: B2C invoices also mandatory via SDI. Some exceptions for very small taxpayers under simplified regime.
ereporting_scope: Domestic: automatic via SDI e-invoicing. Cross-border: Italian company must report via SDI in FatturaPA. Receiving cross-border: self-invoice (autofattura) must be created and sent via SDI.
ereporting_notes: Until Jul 1, 2022, cross-border transactions were reported via Esterometro (monthly/quarterly). Now replaced by SDI submission.
archiving_rules: Conservazione Sostitutiva (AgID guidelines). Annual packages digitally signed. Conservation Manager (Responsabile della Conservazione) must be appointed. Conservation Manual required.
archiving_platform: Internal or delegated to accredited 3rd party
archiving_notes: Both sender and recipient must archive. Packages must be digitally signed/sealed annually. AgID-compliant procedures required.

name: France
b2g_network: Chorus Pro; from 2026 also via Accredited Platforms (PDP)
b2g_notes: All suppliers to public entities must use Chorus Pro. SIRET number required as recipient identifier.
b2b_platform: Accredited Platforms (PDP - Plateformes de Dématérialisation Partenaires) + central PPF
b2b_network: Decentralized Y-model: PDP (accredited private providers) + PPF (Portail Public de Facturation)
b2b_notes: Currently (before mandate): paper, PDF with e-signature/audit trail, EDI accepted. From Sep 2026 large companies must send/receive electronically. All companies must accept from Sep 2026.
b2c_format: Any (e-reporting aggregated data to tax authority)
b2c_notes: No e-invoice obligation for B2C. E-reporting of B2C transaction data to DGFiP via PDP required on same timeline as B2B.
ereporting_scope: International B2B transactions, all B2C transactions. E-invoice data (status lifecycle) also reported for domestic B2B.
ereporting_notes: E-reporting covers cross-border B2B and all B2C. Invoice lifecycle statuses (received, rejected, paid) also reported.
archiving_rules: French Commercial Code & Tax Code. Electronic archiving must ensure authenticity, integrity and legibility throughout retention period.
archiving_platform: Internal or certified SAE (Système d'Archivage Électronique) providers
archiving_notes: NF Z 42-013 standard recommended. Time-stamping and digital signatures commonly used. PDP may offer archiving services.

name: Germany
b2g_status: Mandatory (acceptance since Nov 2020; sending mandatory varies by level)
b2g_platform: Multiple: OZG-RE (federal), Länder-specific portals; Peppol network
b2g_format: XRechnung (XML, CIUS-DE EN 16931), ZUGFeRD (hybrid PDF/XML), Peppol BIS
b2g_network: Peppol; PEPPOL access points; federal/state-specific portals
b2g_notes: Mandatory to accept at federal level since Nov 2020. At state level: varies (most mandatory by 2021-2022). At local/municipal level: still being rolled out.
b2b_status: Mandatory acceptance since Jan 2025; sending mandatory from 2027-2028
b2b_platform: No central platform. Companies choose their own access point / provider
b2b_format: EN 16931-compliant format required (XRechnung, ZUGFeRD 2.x, Peppol BIS)
b2b_network: Decentralized – any agreed network or direct transmission
b2b_notes: Annual turnover ≤ 800K EUR: sending mandatory from Jan 2028. Currently paper, PDF, EDI all still accepted for sending until mandate kicks in. All companies must be able to RECEIVE e-invoices since Jan 2025.
b2c_notes: No e-invoicing or e-reporting obligation for B2C transactions in Germany.
ereporting_scope: No e-reporting obligation currently. Future ViDA-aligned reporting being considered.
ereporting_notes: Germany plans to introduce transaction-based reporting aligned with EU ViDA directive, timeline TBD.
archiving_rules: HGB (Commercial Code) §257 + AO (Tax Code) §147. GoBD principles apply: traceability, unchangeability, completeness.
archiving_notes: GoBD compliance required. Electronic invoices must be stored in original electronic format. ZUGFeRD/XRechnung XML must be kept, not just PDF visual.

name: Spain
b2g_platform: FACe (central registry); regional portals (FACeB2B for regions)
b2g_format: Facturae (XML, Spanish national format); also EN 16931/UBL
b2g_notes: All invoices to public administrations must go through FACe or approved regional platforms. Facturae format with digital signature required.
b2b_since: Large (>8M€ turnover): ~2025; SMEs: ~2026 (exact dates pending secondary legislation)
b2b_platform: SPFE (Sistema Público de Facturas Electrónicas) / Accredited private platforms
b2b_network: Decentralized with public hub interconnection (similar to France PDP model)
b2b_notes: Crea y Crece Law (Law 18/2022) mandates B2B e-invoicing. Secondary legislation (reglamento) still pending final publication as of Jun 2026. Also existing SII system for VAT reporting (large taxpayers).
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing mandate. B2C transactions covered by SII VAT reporting for large taxpayers.
ereporting_status: Mandatory (SII – Suministro Inmediato de Información) for large taxpayers
ereporting_since: Jul 1, 2017 (turnover >6M€); Jan 1, 2018 (VAT groups); voluntary for others
ereporting_scope: Real-time VAT ledger reporting: sales/purchase invoices, intra-EU operations, capital assets. All invoices must be reported within 4 days of issue.
ereporting_frequency: Within 4 calendar days of invoice issue/receipt
ereporting_notes: SII is a separate obligation from e-invoicing. Under new SPFE system, B2B e-invoicing and e-reporting will be unified. Cross-border transactions also covered by SII.
archiving_retention: 4 years (tax statute of limitations) / up to 10 years recommended
archiving_rules: General Tax Law Art. 29. Electronic invoices must be kept in original electronic form with digital signature. Royal Decree 1619/2012 on invoicing.
archiving_notes: Electronic invoices with advanced e-signature or EDI + audit trail satisfy authenticity requirement. Storage in Spain not mandatory but must be accessible to AEAT.

name: Belgium
b2g_notes: All federal public entities must receive Peppol e-invoices. Regional/local administrations integrated progressively.
b2b_notes: All Belgian VAT-registered companies must send/receive structured e-invoices via Peppol from Jan 1, 2026. Small companies (below VAT threshold) exempt.
b2c_notes: No e-invoicing mandate for B2C. Standard VAT obligations apply.
ereporting_scope: No specific e-reporting beyond e-invoicing mandate. ViDA transposition expected.
ereporting_notes: Belgian e-invoicing mandate focuses on exchange; separate e-reporting not yet mandated.
archiving_rules: Belgian VAT Code Art. 60. Electronic invoices must be stored ensuring authenticity and integrity. Original electronic format must be preserved.
archiving_notes: Peppol access points may offer archiving. XML original must be kept alongside any PDF.

name: Netherlands
b2g_format: Peppol BIS 3.0 (NLCIUS – Dutch CIUS of EN 16931, UBL 2.1)
b2g_notes: EU Directive 2014/55/EU implemented. All central government entities must receive Peppol e-invoices. Sub-central (municipalities, provinces) voluntary but widely adopted.
b2b_notes: No B2B mandate. Market adoption via Peppol is high. ViDA implementation will bring future obligations.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing requirement.
ereporting_scope: Standard VAT returns. ViDA transposition may introduce future e-reporting.
ereporting_notes: Netherlands awaits ViDA implementation for any future e-reporting mandate.
archiving_rules: Dutch Tax Law (AWR Art. 52). Electronic invoices may be stored electronically. Integrity and authenticity must be guaranteed.
archiving_notes: Tax authority must be able to access records. Foreign storage allowed if accessible within reasonable time.

name: Poland
b2g_platform: PEF (Platforma Elektronicznego Fakturowania) – national Peppol authority
b2g_format: PEPPOL BIS 3.0 (UBL/CII), local PEPPOL CIUS (KSeF-compatible formats)
b2g_notes: All contracting authorities must accept e-invoices. PEF is national broker for Peppol delivery.
b2b_platform: KSeF (Krajowy System e-Faktur) – national clearance platform
b2b_format: FA_VAT (national XML schema – Polish structured invoice format)
b2b_notes: KSeF assigns unique invoice identifier. Invoice number from KSeF is legally binding. API-based submission. Mandatory for VAT-registered companies (B2B domestic). Consumer invoices (B2C) excluded.
b2c_notes: B2C invoices excluded from KSeF mandate. Standard cash register/receipt obligations apply.
ereporting_scope: All domestic B2B invoices automatically reported to tax authority via KSeF clearance. Cross-border still under standard VAT return.
ereporting_notes: KSeF provides tax authority real-time access to all B2B invoice data. JPK_VAT (SAF-T) reporting also mandatory monthly.
archiving_retention: 5 years (tax) from end of year of tax obligation
archiving_rules: Tax Ordinance Art. 86. KSeF stores invoices for 10 years (government archiving). Companies may rely on KSeF for archiving or maintain own copies.
archiving_notes: KSeF retains all e-invoices for 10 years. Taxpayers may reference KSeF as their archive. FA_VAT format must be preserved.

name: Portugal
b2g_platform: eSPAP (Entidade de Serviços Partilhados da AP) / ESPAP portal
b2g_notes: All suppliers to central government must issue CIUS-PT e-invoices. Regional/local entities being integrated progressively.
b2b_since: Certified software mandatory since 2010; SAF-T reporting ongoing
b2b_platform: No central platform for B2B; SAF-T reporting to AT (Tax Authority)
b2b_format: Certified billing software output (XML/SAF-T); PDF invoices widely used
b2b_notes: Portugal requires certified invoicing software (ATCUD code mandatory). All invoices must have unique ATCUD code. No structured e-invoice exchange mandate for B2B yet.
b2c_status: ATCUD code mandatory; e-reporting via SAF-T
b2c_notes: All invoices (including B2C) require ATCUD code. Monthly SAF-T submission mandatory.
ereporting_scope: Monthly SAF-T (PT) file submission: sales/purchase invoices, receipts, transport docs. All companies with turnover > threshold.
ereporting_notes: SAF-T submission is the primary e-reporting mechanism. ATCUD codes allow AT to track all invoices in real time.
archiving_rules: Portuguese Tax Code (CIVA Art. 52). Electronic invoices must be preserved in original format ensuring authenticity and integrity.
archiving_notes: SAF-T files must also be kept. AT must be able to access records within 10-day notice.

name: Romania
b2g_platform: SPV (Spațiul Privat Virtual) – RO e-Factura system
b2g_notes: All B2G invoices must be submitted via RO e-Factura. Government validates and assigns unique ID.
b2b_since: Jan 1, 2024 (real-time reporting Jan 2024; mandatory exchange from Jan 2024)
b2b_notes: All domestic B2B invoices must be submitted to RO e-Factura within 5 days of issue. System assigns unique e-Invoice ID. Recipient downloads from ANAF platform.
b2c_notes: B2C transaction data must be reported to ANAF. Specific e-invoicing for B2C introduced as part of broader compliance package.
ereporting_scope: All domestic B2B and B2C transactions. Real-time reporting via clearance. Cross-border still covered by VAT returns.
ereporting_frequency: Within 5 calendar days of invoice date
ereporting_notes: Fines apply for non-submission. ANAF has real-time visibility of all domestic invoices. RO e-SAF-T also running in parallel.
archiving_rules: Romanian Fiscal Code. Electronic invoices must be stored ensuring authenticity, integrity, and readability. Electronic signature or audit trail required.
archiving_platform: Internal or certified providers; ANAF retains copies
archiving_notes: ANAF retains all e-invoices submitted. Companies should maintain own copies. PDF+XML format recommended.

name: Greece
b2g_platform: myDATA (IAPR – Independent Authority for Public Revenue)
b2g_format: Greek XML INVOICE format via myDATA API
b2g_notes: B2G invoices must be transmitted and reported via myDATA. Public administrations receive via ESYP platform.
b2b_status: Mandatory e-reporting (myDATA); structured invoice exchange in progress
b2b_format: Greek XML format (myDATA API) or via certified ERP/provider
b2b_notes: All B2B invoices must be reported to myDATA in real time. E-invoicing (structured exchange between parties) is separate and growing. Greek e-invoicing model focuses on reporting, not necessarily on structured exchange.
b2c_notes: Retail/B2C transactions summarized and reported via myDATA. POS cash register data feeds myDATA.
ereporting_scope: All income/expense data (sales, purchases, payroll, depreciation) reported via myDATA. Real-time for sales invoices.
ereporting_frequency: Real-time (per invoice for sales); monthly/quarterly for expenses
ereporting_notes: myDATA is a comprehensive accounting data platform. Non-compliance penalized. Pre-filled VAT returns generated from myDATA data.
archiving_rules: Greek Tax Code (Law 4174/2013). Electronic documents must maintain authenticity and integrity. myDATA provides government-side retention.
archiving_notes: myDATA acts as a de facto government archive. Companies must also maintain own records for the statutory period.

name: Australia
b2g_notes: Federal government agencies must receive Peppol e-invoices. States/territories adopting progressively.
b2b_notes: No B2B mandate. Government encourages Peppol adoption. 5-day payment rule incentivizes e-invoicing for SMEs.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing requirement.
ereporting_status: Not specific e-reporting mandate (standard BAS/GST reporting)
ereporting_scope: Standard BAS (Business Activity Statement) quarterly/monthly. No real-time invoice reporting.
ereporting_notes: ATO exploring digital reporting enhancements. No transaction-level reporting yet.
archiving_rules: Tax Administration Act 1953. Records must be kept in English or accessible form. Electronic format acceptable.
archiving_notes: ATO accepts electronic records. No specific e-archiving standard mandated.

name: Austria
b2g_notes: One of first EU countries with B2G mandate. Federal agencies: ebInterface or Peppol. States/municipalities: mostly adopted.
b2b_notes: No B2B mandate. ebInterface widely used in private sector voluntarily. ViDA will bring future obligations.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing requirement.
ereporting_scope: Standard VAT returns. No real-time invoice reporting mandate.
ereporting_notes: Austria awaits ViDA implementation.
archiving_rules: Federal Fiscal Code (BAO §132). Electronic invoices stored electronically must guarantee authenticity and integrity.
archiving_notes: ebInterface XML files must be preserved. Audit trail must be maintained.

name: Denmark
b2g_notes: Denmark was the first country in the world to mandate B2G e-invoicing (2005). All public entities must receive e-invoices via NemHandel/Peppol.
b2b_notes: No B2B mandate but extremely high voluntary adoption. Many large companies require e-invoices from suppliers.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing requirement.
ereporting_scope: Standard quarterly VAT returns. No real-time invoice reporting.
ereporting_notes: Denmark may implement ViDA in future.
archiving_rules: Danish Bookkeeping Act. Electronic records must be kept in original format ensuring readability and authenticity.
archiving_notes: From 2024: new Danish Bookkeeping Act requires digital bookkeeping for most companies.

name: Finland
b2g_format: Finvoice 3.0 (Finnish national standard); Peppol BIS 3.0; TEAPPSXML
b2g_notes: One of the most advanced e-invoicing markets in Europe. Finvoice delivered via Finnish banking network is the primary standard.
b2b_status: Highly voluntary (near-mandatory in practice for large companies)
b2b_notes: No formal B2B mandate but near-100% adoption among large companies. Finnish banking network provides universal invoice delivery.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing requirement.
ereporting_scope: Standard VAT returns. No real-time invoice reporting.
ereporting_notes: Finland will implement ViDA in future.
archiving_rules: Finnish Accounting Act. Electronic invoices may be stored electronically. Integrity must be maintained.
archiving_notes: Electronic storage widely adopted. Banking network operators often provide archiving services.

name: Sweden
b2g_notes: EU Directive 2014/55/EU transposed. All public entities must receive Peppol e-invoices. SVEFAKTURA being phased out.
b2b_notes: No B2B mandate. High voluntary adoption especially in large corporates.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing requirement.
ereporting_scope: Standard VAT returns. No real-time invoice reporting.
ereporting_notes: Sweden will implement ViDA directive in future.
archiving_rules: Swedish Bookkeeping Act (BFL). Electronic originals must be preserved. Conversion to another format may require keeping both.
archiving_notes: Electronic archiving accepted. Original electronic format must be retained if legally significant.

name: Norway
b2g_notes: Norway was among first to mandate B2G e-invoicing. EHF format based on Peppol BIS. ELMA is national address registry.
b2b_since: Jan 1, 2027 (sending large companies); Jan 1, 2028 (all sending); Jan 1, 2030 (all reception)
b2b_notes: Norwegian Parliament approved B2B e-invoicing mandate in Jun 2026. Phased rollout 2027-2030. Applies to VAT-registered companies.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing mandate planned.
ereporting_status: Not mandatory (SAF-T required for tax audits)
ereporting_scope: SAF-T Norway must be available on request during tax audits. No real-time reporting.
ereporting_notes: SAF-T NO format required for companies with electronic accounting systems. No real-time transaction reporting yet.
archiving_rules: Norwegian Accounting Act. Electronic invoices must be stored ensuring authenticity and readability.
archiving_notes: Electronic archiving accepted. Must be accessible to tax authority.

name: Ireland
b2g_notes: Government bodies connected to Peppol. Suppliers encouraged to send e-invoices. Full mandate still being finalised.
b2b_notes: Revenue (Irish tax authority) preparing B2B e-invoicing mandate ahead of EU ViDA. Timeline: potentially 2028. Consultation ongoing as of 2026.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing requirement currently.
ereporting_scope: Standard VAT3 returns. No real-time invoice reporting.
ereporting_notes: Ireland may introduce e-reporting aligned with ViDA. Real-time reporting under discussion.
archiving_rules: Taxes Consolidation Act 1997. Electronic records must be kept in format that allows inspection by Revenue.
archiving_notes: Electronic records widely accepted. No specific e-archiving standard mandated.

name: Croatia
b2g_notes: All invoices to public bodies must go through FINA eRačun. FINA (Financial Agency) is Croatian Peppol authority.
b2b_notes: Croatia extended mandate to B2B. VAT-registered companies must send/receive structured e-invoices. FINA acts as central hub.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing mandate.
ereporting_scope: All domestic B2B invoices visible to tax authority via FINA platform.
ereporting_notes: FINA provides tax authority visibility. Standard VAT returns also required.
archiving_rules: Croatian VAT Act. Electronic invoices must be stored ensuring authenticity, integrity, readability.
archiving_notes: Digital signature or EDI/audit trail method must be used. Electronic storage accepted.

name: India
b2g_status: Mandatory (via e-invoicing system for eligible taxpayers)
b2g_format: JSON (GST schema / e-invoice schema); PDF with QR code
b2g_notes: Threshold-based: turnover >5 crore INR (Aug 2023+). IRN (Invoice Reference Number) and QR code mandatory.
b2b_since: Oct 1, 2020 (>500 Cr); threshold progressively lowered to >5 Cr from Aug 2023
b2b_format: JSON (GST e-invoice schema); PDF with IRN+QR code for sharing
b2b_notes: Invoice submitted to IRP, validated, gets IRN and QR code. Must be shared with buyer within 24h. B2B only (B2C exempted above threshold).
b2c_status: QR code mandatory (B2C > 500 Cr turnover); e-invoicing not required
b2c_notes: B2C invoices not submitted to IRP. Dynamic QR code mandatory for large taxpayers on B2C invoices.
ereporting_scope: GSTR-1 (outward supplies), GSTR-3B (summary return), GSTR-2A/2B (auto-populated purchase). E-invoice data auto-populates GSTR-1.
ereporting_frequency: Monthly (GSTR-1 and 3B); some quarterly for small taxpayers
ereporting_notes: E-invoice IRN auto-populates GST returns, reducing duplication. E-way bill system for goods transport also mandatory.
archiving_retention: 8 years (from due date of annual return)
archiving_rules: GST Act Section 36. Electronic records acceptable. IRN issued by IRP is official record.
archiving_notes: GSTN retains e-invoice data. Taxpayers must maintain books of accounts for 8 years. Electronic format accepted.

name: Malaysia
b2g_notes: B2G and B2B use the same MyInvois platform. Government is major early adopter.
b2b_format: XML or JSON (LHDN MyInvois schema); UBL 2.1 or CII based
b2b_notes: Clearance model: invoice submitted to LHDN, validated, QR code assigned. Buyer must acknowledge. 72-hour rejection window.
b2c_format: XML or JSON (simplified invoice for B2C)
b2c_notes: Simplified e-invoice for B2C transactions. Some exemptions for very small businesses.
ereporting_scope: All invoices (B2B, B2C, B2G) cleared through MyInvois, providing LHDN real-time data. Standard tax returns also required.
ereporting_notes: LHDN has real-time visibility of all invoices. Separate SST reporting also required.
archiving_rules: Income Tax Act / GST Act. Electronic records must be stored securely ensuring accessibility and integrity.
archiving_notes: MyInvois platform retains e-invoices. Taxpayers should maintain own records. QR code-validated copy is official.

name: Mexico
b2g_platform: SAT (Servicio de Administración Tributaria) via PAC (Authorized Certifiers)
b2g_format: CFDI 4.0 (XML – Comprobante Fiscal Digital por Internet)
b2g_notes: B2G invoices use the same CFDI system as B2B. Government agencies receive CFDI from PAC.
b2b_platform: PAC (Proveedores Autorizados de Certificación) – authorized certifiers
b2b_network: PAC clearance model (PAC validates and certifies; SAT has real-time access)
b2b_notes: One of oldest e-invoicing mandates globally. All invoices must be cleared through a PAC. SAT has real-time access to all CFDI data. CFDI 4.0 requires buyer's RFC and address.
b2c_status: Mandatory (simplified CFDI or global CFDI for retail)
b2c_since: 2011 (progressive); Global CFDI for retail common practice
b2c_notes: Businesses may issue global CFDI summarizing daily B2C transactions. Individual B2C CFDI on request.
ereporting_scope: All CFDI data (B2B, B2B, B2G) is transmitted to SAT in real-time via PAC. SAT has complete picture of all invoicing.
ereporting_notes: Mexico has one of most comprehensive real-time reporting systems globally. DIOT for foreign supplier services also required.
archiving_rules: SAT rules and Fiscal Code. CFDI XML must be kept. SAT retains copies.
archiving_platform: SAT retains; PAC may offer archiving; internal
archiving_notes: SAT has full archive of all CFDIs. Taxpayers should maintain their own copies. PDF+XML (Addenda) is common delivery format.

name: Saudi Arabia
b2g_since: Dec 4, 2021 (Phase 1 – generation); 2022 onwards (Phase 2 – clearance for large)
b2g_platform: ZATCA (Zakat, Tax and Customs Authority) – Fatoora platform
b2g_notes: Phase 1 (Dec 2021): all taxpayers must generate and store e-invoices. Phase 2: clearance via ZATCA for large taxpayers (wave-based rollout).
b2b_since: Phase 2 wave 1: Jan 1, 2023 (>3B SAR turnover); successive waves through 2025+
b2b_format: UBL 2.1 XML (structured); simplified invoice for B2C
b2b_notes: Wave-based integration. Each wave targets companies above specific turnover threshold. Clearance: invoice sent to ZATCA, validated, UUID and QR assigned, then shared with buyer.
b2c_status: Simplified e-invoice mandatory (Phase 1 generation, Phase 2 reporting)
b2c_notes: Simplified e-invoices for B2C. Phase 2 requires reporting simplified invoices to ZATCA. QR code mandatory on all simplified invoices.
ereporting_scope: Phase 1: invoice generation & storage. Phase 2: real-time clearance for B2B (standard invoices) and near-real-time reporting for B2C (simplified invoices).
ereporting_notes: ZATCA has complete real-time visibility. VAT return still required separately.
archiving_rules: ZATCA Regulations. E-invoices must be stored in original XML format. ZATCA may audit at any time.
archiving_notes: ZATCA recommends ISO 27001-certified archiving. UUID from ZATCA is primary reference. Tamper-proof storage required.

name: Colombia
b2g_platform: DIAN (Dirección de Impuestos y Aduanas Nacionales)
b2g_notes: B2G uses same DIAN e-invoicing system. Public entities must receive DIAN-validated invoices.
b2b_since: 2019 (large); 2022 (all taxpayers subject to income tax or VAT)
b2b_network: DIAN (clearance model via technology providers – OFEs)
b2b_notes: Invoice cleared via DIAN. Unique CUFE code assigned. Colombia also has e-payroll (nómina electrónica) and e-document equivalents.
b2c_notes: POS (cash registers) must issue electronic equivalent documents via DIAN. Progressive rollout by sector.
ereporting_scope: All e-invoices (B2B, B2G) and equivalent documents cleared through DIAN. Real-time government visibility.
ereporting_notes: DIAN has comprehensive real-time invoice data. Exogenous information (annual declaration) also required.
archiving_rules: Colombian Tax Code. XML originals must be preserved. DIAN retains copies.
archiving_notes: DIAN stores all validated invoices. Companies must maintain own records. CUFE is primary reference.

name: Israel
b2g_notes: Government invoices managed through ITA system.
b2b_status: Clearance mandatory for invoices >25,000 ILS (from May 2024)
b2b_format: XML or structured data via ITA API
b2b_notes: From May 2024: invoices above 25,000 ILS must receive a clearance number (Mispar Haktzaa) from ITA before being sent to buyer. Threshold progressively decreasing to 0 ILS by 2028.
b2c_status: Not specifically required (B2B clearance threshold applies)
b2c_notes: No specific B2C e-invoicing. Standard receipts/invoices apply.
ereporting_scope: Invoice data above threshold transmitted to ITA for clearance number issuance. ITA has real-time visibility.
archiving_rules: Israeli Tax Ordinance. Electronic records must be kept in accessible format. Clearance numbers must be preserved.
archiving_notes: Clearance number (Mispar Haktzaa) is key reference for archiving. Electronic storage accepted.

name: Singapore
b2g_notes: Government agencies integrated with InvoiceNow. Budget 2025 incentives for SMEs to adopt e-invoicing.
b2b_since: Nov 1, 2025 (GST-registered businesses must be able to receive InvoiceNow)
b2b_notes: IRAS and IMDA joint initiative. GST-registered businesses: mandatory InvoiceNow reception from Nov 2025. Sending encouraged with grants/incentives.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing mandate.
ereporting_scope: Standard quarterly GST returns (F5). No real-time invoice reporting.
ereporting_notes: Singapore exploring data analytics from InvoiceNow for compliance. No real-time reporting mandate yet.
archiving_rules: Income Tax Act / GST Act. Electronic records must be kept accessible and readable.
archiving_notes: IRAS accepts electronic records. No specific e-archiving standard mandated.

name: New Zealand
b2g_since: Mar 31, 2022 (core government agencies); Oct 31, 2022 (all public sector)
b2g_platform: Peppol (MBIE as Peppol Authority, shared with Australia)
b2g_notes: New Zealand and Australia share Peppol governance (PINT A-NZ). All public sector entities must receive Peppol e-invoices.
b2b_notes: No B2B mandate. Government actively promotes Peppol adoption. 20-day payment target incentivizes e-invoicing.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing requirement.
ereporting_scope: Standard GST returns. No real-time invoice reporting.
ereporting_notes: No e-reporting mandate. NZ follows Australia's approach closely.
archiving_rules: Tax Administration Act 1994. Electronic records acceptable if readable and authentic.
archiving_notes: IRD accepts electronic records. No specific standard mandated.

name: United Arab Emirates
b2g_platform: UAE Ministry of Finance (FTA) – pilot platform
b2g_notes: UAE announced e-invoicing roadmap. B2G pilot ongoing. No mandatory date confirmed yet.
b2b_notes: UAE published e-invoicing strategy 2023. Mandatory B2B e-invoicing anticipated. Phase 1 may focus on large taxpayers.
b2c_notes: No B2C mandate yet. May follow Saudi Arabia model.
ereporting_scope: Quarterly VAT returns. No real-time invoice reporting yet.
ereporting_notes: UAE introduced VAT in 2018. E-reporting not yet mandated. Will accompany e-invoicing rollout.
archiving_rules: UAE VAT Law Federal Decree No. 8/2017. Records must be maintained in Arabic or with Arabic translation.
archiving_notes: Electronic records accepted. FTA may request records within 5 business days.

name: United Kingdom
b2g_notes: UK public sector entities must accept e-invoices. Many use Peppol via Crown Commercial Service framework.
b2b_notes: No B2B e-invoicing mandate. Making Tax Digital (MTD) requires digital record-keeping and digital VAT returns but not structured e-invoice exchange.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing requirement.
ereporting_scope: MTD for VAT: digital VAT returns submitted via compatible software. MTD for ITSA (Income Tax Self-Assessment) planned from Apr 2026. No real-time invoice-level reporting.
ereporting_notes: MTD is digital record-keeping + digital VAT returns, not invoice-level reporting. UK post-Brexit diverging from EU ViDA approach.
archiving_rules: HMRC guidance. MTD requires digital records to be kept in functional compatible software. Electronic invoices accepted.
archiving_notes: MTD requires records kept digitally in compatible software. Physical paper invoices can be digitized and original discarded.

name: USA
b2g_platform: IPP (Invoice Processing Platform – Treasury); PIEE (DoD); GSA procurement systems
b2g_format: Multiple (EDI X12, cXML, PDF); Peppol piloted by some states
b2g_notes: Federal level: DoD suppliers required to use PIEE. Treasury uses IPP for civilian agencies. No single federal format. State level: highly fragmented.
b2b_format: EDI X12 810 (widely used); cXML; PDF; Peppol (growing)
b2b_notes: No federal B2B e-invoicing mandate. Large companies often require e-invoicing from suppliers via their procurement systems. DBNAlliance piloting Peppol expansion.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing mandate. Standard receipt/invoice practices apply.
ereporting_scope: Standard tax returns (Form 1120, 1099 series, sales tax returns). No real-time invoice reporting.
ereporting_notes: US has no national VAT, making EU-style e-reporting less applicable. State sales tax reporting varies widely.
archiving_retention: 3-7 years (varies by record type and state)
archiving_rules: IRS Publication 583, state laws. Electronic records must be accurate, complete, accessible. IRS Rev. Proc. 98-25 for electronic records.
archiving_notes: IRS accepts electronic records. No specific e-archiving standard mandated federally. SOX compliance requires 7-year retention for public companies.

name: Oman
b2g_notes: Oman announced e-invoicing initiative. Pilot phases underway. Full mandate timeline TBD.
b2b_notes: Oman VAT introduced in Apr 2021. E-invoicing roadmap being developed. Likely to follow Saudi Arabia/UAE model.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing mandate yet.
ereporting_scope: Quarterly VAT returns. No real-time reporting yet.
ereporting_notes: E-reporting will accompany e-invoicing rollout.
archiving_rules: Oman VAT Law. Records must be kept in original or accessible form.
archiving_notes: Electronic records accepted. OTA may request records on audit.

name: Philippines
b2g_status: Mandatory (eFPS/eBIRForms for tax; EIS for e-invoicing)
b2g_notes: BIR EIS mandatory for large taxpayers. Government procurement via PhilGEPS.
b2b_since: Jul 2022 (top 100 large taxpayers pilot); expanding progressively
b2b_platform: BIR EIS (Bureau of Internal Revenue Electronic Invoicing System)
b2b_notes: EIS transmits invoice data to BIR in real time. Not a clearance model – invoice can be issued but must be reported. Covers receipts and invoices.
b2c_notes: Official receipts (B2C) also covered under EIS. Cashier receipts must be reported.
ereporting_scope: All sales invoices and official receipts of covered taxpayers transmitted to BIR in real time.
ereporting_notes: BIR EIS is a reporting system. Alpha-list and VAT returns also required separately.
archiving_rules: NIRC (National Internal Revenue Code). Electronic records must be kept accessible. BIR must be able to access records.
archiving_notes: BIR retains copies via EIS. Taxpayers must maintain own records for 10 years.

name: Luxembourg
b2g_notes: Phased rollout prioritized larger enterprises first. Manual submission also available through the Guichet.lu public portal.
b2b_format: Paper, PDF with e-signature, or EDI
b2b_notes: No B2B e-invoicing mandate currently. ViDA directive (expected 2030) may introduce future obligations.
b2c_notes: No B2C e-invoicing requirement.
ereporting_scope: No e-reporting mandate. Standard VAT returns apply.
ereporting_notes: Luxembourg awaits ViDA transposition for future e-reporting obligations.
archiving_rules: Standard Luxembourg archiving obligations. Electronic invoices must be stored ensuring authenticity and integrity.
archiving_notes: Electronic storage accepted. Peppol access points may offer archiving services.
